name: Stations
statement:
There are $n$ cities in a row numbered from $1$ to $n$.
The cities will be building broadcasting stations. The station in the $i$-th city has height $h_i$ and range $w_i$. It can broadcast information to city $j$ if the following constraints are met:
‚ÄĘ  $i \le j \le w_i$, and
‚ÄĘ  for each $k$ such that $i  \lt  k \le j$, the following condition holds: $h_k  \lt  h_i$.  In other words, the station in city $i$ can broadcast information to city $j$ if $j \ge i$, $j$ is in the range of $i$-th station, and $i$ is strictly highest on the range from $i$ to $j$ (including city $j$).
At the beginning, for every city $i$, $h_i = 0$ and $w_i = i$.
Then $q$ events will take place. During $i$-th event one of the following will happen:
‚ÄĘ  City $c_i$ will rebuild its station so that its height will be strictly highest among all stations and $w_{c_i}$ will be set to $g_i$.
‚ÄĘ  Let $b_j$ be the number of stations that can broadcast information to city $j$. Print the sum of $b_j$ over all $j$ satisfying $l_i \le j \le r_i$.
Your task is to react to all events and print answers to all queries.
input:
The first line contains two integers $n$ and $q$ ($1 \le n, q \le 2\cdot10^5$) ‚ÄĒ number of cities and number of events.
Then $q$ lines follow. The $i$-th line begins with an integer $p_i$ ($p_i = 1$ or $p_i = 2$).
If $p_i = 1$ a station will be rebuilt. Then two integers $c_i$ and $g_i$ ($1 \le c_i \le g_i \le n$) follow ‚ÄĒ the city in which the station is rebuilt and its new broadcasting range.
If $p_i = 2$ you are given a query. Then two integers $l_i$ and $r_i$ ($1 \le l_i \le r_i \le n$) follow ‚ÄĒ the range of cities in the query.
output: For each query, print in a single line the sum of $b_j$ over the given interval.
